SEARCH
Package com.nib.commons.io.vsam

Class KeySequentialFile

java.lang.Object
com.nib.commons.io.AbstractFile
com.nib.commons.io.vsam.AbstractVsamFile
com.nib.commons.io.vsam.AbstractKeySequentialFile
com.nib.commons.io.vsam.KeySequentialFile
public class KeySequentialFile extends AbstractKeySequentialFile
Represents a key-sequential file object, facilitating operations such as reading, writing, and modifying records within the file. This class provides functionality for handling files with key-based access and supports various modes of operation, including sequential and random access.

KeySequentialFile utilizes a database connection for performing I/O operations. It supports variable-length records, alternate record keys, file status tracking, and error handling. Records can be managed using keys for efficient access.

This class extends AbstractKeySequentialFile, inheriting its base operations. Additional file-specific configurations, such as caching, exceptions, and record key definitions, can be applied to the file through a series of chained methods.

Note: The class relies on an internal I/O API (ioapi) for executing low-level file operations. This API is configured during initialization and operations.

    • initIoApi

      public void initIoApi()
      Initializes the internal I/O API for the current instance of KeySequentialFile.
      Specified by:
      initIoApi in class AbstractVsamFile

    • open

      public FileIOResponse open(OpenMode openMode, LockMode lockMode)
      Opens the key-sequential file with the specified open mode and lock mode.
      Overrides:
      open in class AbstractKeySequentialFile
      Parameters:
      openMode - the mode to open the file with, which determines the type of access (e.g., input, output).
      lockMode - the locking mode to apply to the file, which defines concurrent access behavior.
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object representing the result of the file open operation.

    • read

      public FileIOResponse read(AbstractNField field, boolean equal, boolean update)
      Reads a record from the key-sequential file based on the specified key field and conditions, and optionally updates the record.
      Specified by:
      read in class AbstractKeySequentialFile
      Parameters:
      field - the AbstractNField object representing the key field to be used for the read operation
      equal - a boolean value indicating whether to retrieve a record with a key that equals the specified key field (true) or greater-equal (false)
      update - a boolean value indicating whether the record is to be locked for update (true) or read-only (false)
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object representing the result of the read operation, including the status and any relevant data

    • start

      public FileIOResponse start(AbstractNField field, KeyIs startMode)
      Starts a browse operation at the specified key field using the provided start mode.
      Specified by:
      start in class AbstractKeySequentialFile
      Parameters:
      field - the AbstractNField object representing the key field to be used as the starting point for the operation
      startMode - the KeyIs enumeration value specifying the starting mode for the file operation
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object representing the result of the start operation, including the status and any relevant data

    • last

      public FileIOResponse last(AbstractNField field)
      Retrieves the last record in the file based on the specified field.
      Parameters:
      field - The field used to determine the last record in the file.
      Returns:
      A FileIOResponse object representing the result of the file operation.

    • readSequential

      public FileIOResponse readSequential(KeyIs direction, boolean update)
      Reads a record sequentially in the specified direction from the file. Handles reading operations and manages buffering, record lengths, and key updates as required.
      Specified by:
      readSequential in class AbstractVsamFile
      Parameters:
      direction - specifies the direction in which to read sequentially. It can indicate forward or backward reading depending on the file's structure.
      update - indicates whether the operation should update the current state or key position after the read.
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object that encapsulates the status and information regarding the read operation.

    • write

      public FileIOResponse write()
      Writes the current record from the buffer to the file and handles any errors that may occur during the write operation. Performs validations, updates file status, and resets the record key after a successful operation.
      Specified by:
      write in class AbstractVsamFile
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object that represents the result of the write operation, including details such as the file's current state and status.
      Throws:
      FileNotOpenForWriteException - if the file is not opened for writing.
      FileIOException - if an error occurs during the file operation, such as an invalid record length.

    • rewrite

      public FileIOResponse rewrite()
      Rewrites the current record in the file, replacing the existing one. This method ensures that the file is open for writing and that a read operation was performed before the rewrite attempt. It validates the record length and overwrites the record associated with the current key.
      Specified by:
      rewrite in class AbstractVsamFile
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse indicating the result of the rewrite operation, including status and any relevant file details
      Throws:
      FileNotOpenForWriteException - if the file is not open for writing
      FileIOException - if the rewrite operation is attempted before a read operation, or if an invalid record length is detected
      IllegalRecordLengthException - if the record length does not match the expected length

    • delete

      public FileIOResponse delete()
      Deletes a record from the file based on the primary key field. This method ensures that the file is open for write operations before proceeding with the delete operation. If the file is not open for write, an exception is thrown. The method attempts to delete the key using the provided key definition and key buffer. Upon successful deletion, the file status is updated. Any errors encountered during the operation are handled and passed to the error-handling logic.
      Specified by:
      delete in class AbstractVsamFile
      Returns:
      A FileIOResponse object containing the status and context of the delete operation.

    • deleteGeneric

      public FileIOResponse deleteGeneric(AbstractNField key, int len)
      Deletes all records matching the provided key and for the provided length.
      Specified by:
      deleteGeneric in class AbstractKeySequentialFile
      Parameters:
      key - the key to be deleted
      len - the length of the key to match
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object containing the result of the delete operation

    • close

      public FileIOResponse close()
      Closes the file and releases associated resources. Resets internal keys, closes any active I/O operations, and updates the file status.
      Overrides:
      close in class AbstractVsamFile
      Returns:
      a FileIOResponse object representing the result of the close operation.
